evidence.interpretation: a_n / (C * Gamma(n) * zeta_*^(-n)) -> 1 only at the 1/n rate, NOT exponentially. The prompt-016 working-hypothesis that the leading subtraction has an exponentially-small (1/2)^n residual at the 2 zeta_* ladder rung is falsified at this dps level: d=2 PCFs DO carry polynomial-in-1/n corrections to the leading Birkhoff amplitude even though their leading Gamma-argument shift beta_R is zero (T35 measurement).
evidence.phase_C_consequence: R_n := r_n * (2 zeta_*)^n / Gamma(n) diverges like 2^n / n^k because r_n is dominated by polynomial-in-1/n terms, NOT by the next ladder rung. Richardson on R_n produces an apparent R_inf ~ 10^654 (manifest noise amplification); cross-method digit agreement collapses to ~10^-15 digits.

what_was_extracted_instead.method: Richardson + endpoint estimator on s_n := n * (a_n/a_n_lead - 1) -> a_1; ~3-4 stable digits at n in {1500, 1900}
what_was_extracted_instead.why_only_3_digits: the cached series is at dps=250 and Richardson on the polynomial-correction sequence runs out of precision floor; deeper precision requires extending the recurrence at higher dps (out of scope here).

consistency_with_prior_claims.T35_C_amplitudes_unchanged: T35's C values are NOT contradicted; the convention-check failure at the 60-digit level reflects the omitted polynomial-in-1/n corrections, not a falsification of T35's C extraction (which used Richardson on the asymptotically-dominant Gamma(n+beta_R) term and is robust to the polynomial corrections in the COEFFICIENTS of the leading amplitude).
consistency_with_prior_claims.G19_beta_R_zero_unchanged: T35's beta_R = 0 measurement is unaffected; it characterises the leading Gamma-argument shift, not the higher-order coefficients of the asymptotic series.

recommended_next_step_for_prompt_018: Use a polynomial-correction-aware ansatz: jointly fit (a_1, a_2, ..., a_K) with C via constrained-LSQ on T_n = a_n * zeta^n / Gamma(n) over a polynomial-decay window, then form the residual r_n with the FULL polynomial expansion subtracted, and only then apply Richardson at the 2 zeta_* scale. K must be large enough that the polynomial residual at the inner window is below the dps-250 noise floor, i.e. K ~ 30-50.
